symptoms.symptom_name: Reactive triad (asymmetric arthritis + conjunctivitis + urethritis) post-Campylobacter

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Asymmetric lower-limb arthritis with conjunctivitis and urethritis ~3 weeks post-enteric infection is the reactive-arthritis triad.
Multi-system involvement points to a systemic reactive process rather than isolated joint sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warm ankle effusion (hot joint) with low-grade fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A warm effused joint with fever is a hot joint that must be aspirated to exclude infection, even when reactive arthritis is likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single fiery/tense joint, no high fever/rigors, no necrotic pustules

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of necrotic pustules and a fiery tense joint/rigors lowers DGI probability, but aspiration/STI screen are still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gritty red eyes without pus, normal vision (reactive conjunctivitis; uveitis screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral non-purulent conjunctivitis is part of the reactive triad.
Reactive arthritis is associated with sight-threatening anterior uveitis; sudden vision change/eye pain requires emergency ophthalmology.
Urethritis can be reactive or from a concurrent STI; screening is needed to exclude gonococcal/chlamydial infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warm asymmetric ankle effusion with Achilles enthesitis; injected conjunctivae/clear corneas; trace-leukocyte urine (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Enthesitis with asymmetric large-joint arthritis and conjunctivitis fits reactive arthritis.
A warm ankle effusion requires aspiration/synovial analysis to exclude infection.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Reactive arthritis (septic/gonococcal joint and uveitis to exclude)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: An asymmetric warm ankle arthritis with conjunctivitis and urethritis ~3 weeks post-Campylobacter is classic reactive arthritis, but a warm effused joint is a hot joint requiring aspiration to exclude septic/gonococcal arthritis, and reactive disease can involve sight-threatening uveitis — so urgent in-person assessment (± aspiration), STI screening and eye safety-netting are required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person joint assessment and ankle aspiration (synovial microscopy/culture/crystals/Gram stain)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude septic/gonococcal arthritis and crystals in the warm effused joint; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: STI screening (NAAT for gonorrhoea/chlamydia) and bloods (FBC, CRP, ESR)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude concurrent STI/DGI and assess inflammation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Ophthalmology (slit-lamp) if any eye pain/photophobia/vision change
